expectations.text: (e2-file-isolation) Response recommends assigning routes.ts to only one session or otherwise isolating who owns it
expectations.evidence: Option 1 ('Serialize One of the Sessions') explicitly recommends letting one session finish and merge first before the second session touches routes.ts. Option 3 recommends splitting routes.ts into separate files so each session owns a distinct file. The Key Principle section states: 'The most reliable fix is to ensure parallel sessions never need to write to the same file.'

expectations.text: (e2-concrete-options) Response gives at least one concrete, actionable option
expectations.evidence: The response provides four concrete options with step-by-step instructions: (1) Serialize one session — pick a 'first' session, let it commit and merge, then rebase the second worktree; (2) Coordinate by file section if changes are in different areas; (3) Refactor routes.ts into feature-scoped files before parallelizing; (4) Designate an explicit integration branch and resolve the conflict once with full context.

expectations.text: (e2-rebase-or-sequence) Response mentions rebase or merge ordering, or sequencing sessions
expectations.evidence: Option 1 states: 'rebase the second session on top of the updated branch before it proceeds' and 'run git fetch and rebase onto the updated base branch.' Option 2 says 'Plan your merge order: merge the simpler change first, then merge the second branch after a quick rebase.' The Practical Checklist also states: 'serialize: finish and merge session A before session B edits routes.ts.'

eval_feedback: The response without the skill performs very well on all three assertions. It gives thorough, structured advice covering file isolation (assigning ownership or splitting the file), concrete actionable options (four distinct strategies with step-by-step instructions), and explicitly mentions rebase and sequencing/merge ordering. The response is comprehensive and well-organized, addressing both immediate triage and long-term structural fixes.
